All 4 tasks complete. Here's the summary:

## Done

**Replaced GitHub webhook ingestion with Atom feed polling.** 5 commits:

| Commit | Change |
|--------|--------|
| `e5879c4` | `GitHubAtomSource` + tests (4 tests, all passing) |
| `5ac6843` | Code review fixes: rename `url`→`feedURL`, map `release_url` to metadata, drop unused `Title` |
| `9150647` | Register `"github"` case in source loader |
| `3636560` | Delete `github.go` webhook handler, remove webhook route + `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET` from main.go |
| `ca06f51` | Remove stale env var from CLAUDE.md |

**What changed:**
- GitHub sources (`provider = "github"`) now poll `https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}/releases.atom` on the standard 5-minute interval alongside DockerHub sources
- No more webhook endpoint, no more `GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET`
- Release notes come through as `Changelog`, release URL stored in `Metadata["release_url"]`
- Zero new dependencies — uses Go's `encoding/xml`

1→package ingestion
     2→

     4→	"context"
     5→	"net/http"
     6→	"net/http/httptest"
     7→	"testing"
     8→)
     9→
    10→const sampleAtomFeed = `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    11→<feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
    12→  <title>Release notes from go-ethereum</title>
    13→  <entry>
    14→    <id>tag:github.com,2008:Repository/15452919/v1.17.0</id>
    15→    <updated>2026-02-17T16:31:00Z</updated>
    16→    <title>Eezo-Inlaid Circuitry (v1.17.0)</title>
    17→    <content type="html"><h2>Changes</h2><p>Bug fixes</p></content>
    18→    <link rel="alternate" type="text/html" href="https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.17.0"/>
    19→  </entry>
    20→  <entry>
    21→    <id>tag:github.com,2008:Repository/15452919/v1.16.9</id>
    22→    <updated>2026-02-17T16:06:58Z</updated>
    23→    <title>Shield Focusing Module (v1.16.9)</title>
    24→    <content type="html"><p>Patch release</p></content>
    25→    <link rel="alternate" type="text/html" href="https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.16.9"/>
    26→  </entry>
    27→</feed>`
    28→
    29→func TestGitHubAtomSourceName(t *testing.T) {
    30→	src := NewGitHubAtomSource(http.DefaultClient, "ethereum/go-ethereum", "src-id")
    31→	if got := src.Name(); got != "github" {
    32→		t.Errorf("Name() = %q, want %q", got, "github")
    33→	}
    34→}
    35→
    36→func TestGitHubAtomFetchNewReleases(t *testing.T) {
    37→	srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    38→		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/atom+xml")
    39→		w.Write([]byte(sampleAtomFeed))
    40→	}))
    41→	defer srv.Close()
    42→
    43→	src := NewGitHubAtomSource(srv.Client(), "ethereum/go-ethereum", "src-id")
    44→	src.baseURL = srv.URL
    45→
    46→	results, err := src.FetchNewReleases(context.Background())
    47→	if err != nil {
    48→		t.Fatalf("FetchNewReleases: %v", err)
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	if len(results) != 2 {
    52→		t.Fatalf("got %d results, want 2", len(results))
    53→	}
    54→
    55→	// First entry
    56→	if results[0].RawVersion != "v1.17.0" {
    57→		t.Errorf("results[0].RawVersion = %q, want %q", results[0].RawVersion, "v1.17.0")
    58→	}
    59→	if results[0].Repository != "ethereum/go-ethereum" {
    60→		t.Errorf("results[0].Repository = %q, want %q", results[0].Repository, "ethereum/go-ethereum")
    61→	}
    62→	if results[0].Changelog == "" {
    63→		t.Error("results[0].Changelog should not be empty")
    64→	}
    65→	if results[0].Timestamp.IsZero() {
    66→		t.Error("results[0].Timestamp should not be zero")
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	// Second entry
    70→	if results[1].RawVersion != "v1.16.9" {
    71→		t.Errorf("results[1].RawVersion = %q, want %q", results[1].RawVersion, "v1.16.9")
    72→	}
    73→}
    74→
    75→func TestGitHubAtomFetchEmpty(t *testing.T) {
    76→	emptyFeed := `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    77→<feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
    78→  <title>Release notes from new-repo</title>
    79→</feed>`
    80→
    81→	srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    82→		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/atom+xml")
    83→		w.Write([]byte(emptyFeed))
    84→	}))
    85→	defer srv.Close()
    86→
    87→	src := NewGitHubAtomSource(srv.Client(), "org/new-repo", "src-id")
    88→	src.baseURL = srv.URL
    89→
    90→	results, err := src.FetchNewReleases(context.Background())
    91→	if err != nil {
    92→		t.Fatalf("FetchNewReleases: %v", err)
    93→	}
    94→	if len(results) != 0 {
    95→		t.Fatalf("got %d results, want 0", len(results))
    96→	}
    97→}
    98→
    99→func TestGitHubAtomFetchHTTPError(t *testing.T) {
   100→	srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   101→		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
   102→	}))
   103→	defer srv.Close()
   104→
   105→	src := NewGitHubAtomSource(srv.Client(), "org/missing", "src-id")
   106→	src.baseURL = srv.URL
   107→
   108→	_, err := src.FetchNewReleases(context.Background())
   109→	if err == nil {
   110→		t.Fatal("expected error for 404 response")
   111→	}
   112→}

Works perfectly against the real feed. 10 releases from `ethereum/go-ethereum`, all fields populated:

- **Versions** parsed correctly — both semver (`v1.17.0`) and named tags (`fusaka-devnet-2`)
- **Timestamps** all valid, no zeros
- **Changelog** present on every release (ranging from 35 bytes to 58KB)

The test is guarded with `testing.Short()` so it won't run in CI unless explicitly targeted. Let me commit this.

content.content:
1→// web/lib/api/client.ts
     2→import type {
     3→  ApiResponse,
     4→  Project,
     5→  ProjectInput,
     6→  Release,
     7→  Source,
     8→  SourceInput,
     9→  Subscription,
    10→  SubscriptionInput,
    11→  NotificationChannel,
    12→  ChannelInput,
    13→  ContextSource,
    14→  ContextSourceInput,
    15→  SemanticRelease,
    16→  AgentRun,
    17→  HealthStatus,
    18→  Stats,
    19→} from "./types";
    20→
    21→const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    22→
    23→async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    24→  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    25→    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    26→    ...init,
    27→  });
    28→  if (!res.ok) {
    29→    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    30→    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    31→  }
    32→  return res.json();
    33→}
    34→
    35→// --- Projects ---
    36→
    37→export const projects = {
    38→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    39→    request<ApiResponse<Project[]>>(`/projects?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    40→  get: (id: string) =>
    41→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`),
    42→  create: (input: ProjectInput) =>
    43→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>("/projects", {
    44→      method: "POST",
    45→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    46→    }),
    47→  update: (id: string, input: ProjectInput) =>
    48→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`, {
    49→      method: "PUT",
    50→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    51→    }),
    52→  delete: (id: string) =>
    53→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    54→};
    55→
    56→// --- Sources (nested under projects) ---
    57→
    58→export const sources = {
    59→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
    60→    request<ApiResponse<Source[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources?page=${page}`),
    61→  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    62→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {
    63→      method: "POST",
    64→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    65→    }),
    66→  get: (id: string) =>
    67→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`),
    68→  update: (id: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    69→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`, {
    70→      method: "PUT",
    71→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    72→    }),
    73→  delete: (id: string) =>
    74→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    75→};
    76→
    77→// --- Releases ---
    78→
    79→export const releases = {
    80→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    81→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    82→  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>
    83→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}`),
    84→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    85→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    86→  get: (id: string) =>
    87→    request<ApiResponse<Release>>(`/releases/${id}`),
    88→};
    89→
    90→// --- Context Sources ---
    91→
    92→export const contextSources = {
    93→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
    94→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources?page=${page}`),
    95→  create: (projectId: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
    96→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources`, {
    97→      method: "POST",
    98→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    99→    }),
